name: llm-cli-gateway
description: A single-user Model Context Protocol gateway that runs Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Grok, and Mistral CLIs from one MCP endpoint with durable async jobs, session continuity, cache-aware prompting, observability, and personal-appliance setup tooling.
implementation_languages: TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Python, Shell, PLpgSQL, HTML
project_entry_attribution: Verivus OSS Releases and llm-cli-gateway contributors
description_good_justification: README.md describes the project in the opening paragraph and in 'What It Provides Today': https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway#what-it-provides-today. It explains that llm-cli-gateway is an MCP gateway for running Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Grok, and Mistral CLIs from one endpoint, with durable async jobs, session continuity, cache-aware prompting, observability, and setup tooling.
interact_justification: README.md documents install and use paths. package.json points bug reports to https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/issues. CONTRIBUTING.md documents contribution workflow. SECURITY.md documents private vulnerability reporting to security@verivus.com.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents setup, development workflow, code quality, test commands, build commands, review checklist, and LLM contributor workflow: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires contributors to follow BEST_PRACTICES.md, write tests as they code, use TypeScript strict mode, run build/lint/format/test checks, keep tool names snake_case, use actionable errors, avoid stdout logging, and maintain type safety: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md.
floss_license_justification: The project is released under the MIT license. package.json declares "license": "MIT" and the top-level LICENSE file contains the MIT license text.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md provides the primary documentation, including what the gateway is, current capabilities, personal-appliance scope, install/upgrade/uninstall commands, Docker fallback, MCP server usage, available tools, persistence, HTTP transport, troubleshooting, and release verification.
documentation_interface_justification: README.md documents the MCP tool surface and usage. docs/personal-mcp/PRODUCT_CONTRACT.md documents the supported scope and provider-support gates. package.json exports document the package entry points, and setup/status.schema.json documents machine-readable setup status output.
sites_https_justification: The repository, releases, npm package, and BadgeApp evidence URLs are all served over HTTPS. The main project URL is https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues is enabled for bug reports and proposed changes: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/issues. Issue and PR threads are searchable, URL-addressable, and allow public participation through GitHub.
english_justification: README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md, docs/, workflow files, and source comments are written in English.
maintained_justification: The public mirror has active recent maintenance. Recent merged PRs include Scorecard enrollment, security quality fixes, Dependabot path fixes, pip dependency bumps, quality badges, and Sigstore release signing. v1.15.2 was published on 2026-05-29: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/releases/tag/v1.15.2.
repo_track_justification: The repository is hosted on GitHub and uses git, which records what changed, who made each change, and when each change was made.
repo_interim_justification: The repository includes interim versions through public pull-request branches and Dependabot branches. Recent examples include merged PRs #13 through #20 and commits 8a5697a, 506c104, ea860d4, 367e2de, 7536bdd, ce9f25f, 2439b50, 0b68e2a, and 8c51db1: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/pulls?q=is%3Apr.
repo_distributed_justification: The repository uses git, a distributed version-control system.
version_unique_justification: Releases use unique version identifiers such as v1.15.3. package.json and package-lock.json carry matching release versions.
version_semver_justification: The project uses Semantic Versioning style tags and package versions, e.g. v1.15.3.
version_tags_justification: GitHub releases are associated with git tags such as https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/releases/tag/v1.15.2.
release_notes_justification: Release notes are published in GitHub Releases and CHANGELOG.md. v1.15.2 release notes are at https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/releases/tag/v1.15.2 and the changelog is at https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md.
release_notes_vulns_justification: SECURITY.md Disclosure states that reporters are credited in CHANGELOG.md for the release that fixes a finding unless they request otherwise. No public runtime vulnerability fix with a CVE is currently recorded for v1.15.2.
report_process_justification: package.json declares the bug-report URL as https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/issues. CONTRIBUTING.md describes the contribution workflow, and SECURITY.md describes the security-reporting process.
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues is enabled and is the issue tracker: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/issues.
report_responses_justification: The public mirror has no closed public issues yet, so there are no ignored issue reports in the last 2-12 months. SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging vulnerability reports within five business days.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement and dependency update requests are handled through public PRs. Recent merged PRs #13 through #20 show active maintainer response, review, and merge activity.
report_archive_justification: GitHub archives issue, pull-request, and review threads at stable URLs. Current PR and issue lists are public at https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/pulls and https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/issues.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents the vulnerability-reporting process and asks reporters to email security@verivus.com with affected version, component, reproduction, and exploitability scope: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/blob/main/SECURITY.md.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: SECURITY.md provides private email reporting through security@verivus.com and describes the requested contents of a private vulnerability report.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md states: 'We aim to acknowledge reports within five business days.' No public vulnerability reports are currently recorded in the public mirror.
build_justification: package.json defines a working build command, `npm run build`, which runs `tsc -p tsconfig.build.json`. CI runs this build on every push and pull request in .github/workflows/ci.yml.
build_common_tools_justification: The TypeScript package builds with npm and TypeScript. Installer artifacts are built with Go and npm through installer/build-release.sh and .github/workflows/release-installer.yml.
build_floss_tools_justification: The build uses FLOSS tools: Node.js/npm, TypeScript, Go, and GitHub Actions workflows with pinned open-source actions.
test_justification: package.json defines automated tests with Vitest: `npm test` and `npm run test:ci`. CI runs `npm run test:ci` on every push and pull request. Recent release commits have successful CI workflow runs: https://github.com/verivus-oss/llm-cli-gateway/actions/workflows/ci.yml.
test_invocation_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents `npm test`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:session`, `npm run test:integration`, and `npm run test:watch`. package.json exposes the same scripts.
test_most_justification: The repository contains broad unit, integration, regression, parser, session, persistence, async-job, HTTP transport, CLI handler, and provider-specific tests under src/__tests__.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on push and pull_request to main and executes npm ci, npm run build, npm run lint, npm run format:check, npm run test:ci, and npm run security:audit.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md says 'Write tests as you code' and the review checklist requires tests to pass, build/lint/format checks to pass, and coverage to be reviewed for substantial code changes.
tests_are_added_justification: Recent feature and security work added regression tests under src/__tests__, including provider handler tests, upstream contract tests, cache-control regression slices, HTTP transport tests, and review-integrity tests. CI enforces the tests before merge.
tests_documented_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the testing policy and local test commands, including the requirement that new functionality should include tests.
warnings_justification: The project uses TypeScript strict compilation, ESLint with eslint-plugin-security, Prettier, actionlint, zizmor, shellcheck, typos, lychee, OSV Scanner, and gitleaks through CI workflows.
warnings_fixed_justification: CI fails on build errors, lint errors, formatting failures, test failures, and the npm security audit. The security workflow fails on workflow/content/dependency/secret scan findings. Current release commits have green CI, security, SAST, OpenSSF Scorecard, Dependency Graph, Dependabot Updates, npm publish, and installer release runs.
warnings_strict_justification: The security workflow runs actionlint, zizmor, shellcheck, OSV Scanner, gitleaks, typos, and lychee. The CI workflow runs TypeScript build, ESLint, Prettier check, Vitest, and release-security-audit.
know_secure_design_justification: The repository documents secure-design boundaries in SECURITY.md, ENFORCEMENT.md, docs/personal-mcp/PRODUCT_CONTRACT.md, and BEST_PRACTICES.md. Security controls include no-shell child_process.spawn execution, allow-listed CLI binaries, MCP-managed approval gates, session content minimization, auth-token-gated HTTP transport, readonly flight-recorder query enforcement, and durable async-job store invariants.
know_common_errors_justification: SECURITY.md explicitly scopes common vulnerability classes relevant to this software: CLI argument injection, HTTP auth/token handling, session-storage leaks, SQL injection/data tampering, async-job isolation, approval-gate bypass, and supply-chain issues. CI includes tools to catch many of these classes.
crypto_published_justification: The gateway does not implement novel cryptographic protocols. It relies on published mechanisms: HTTPS for delivery, bearer tokens for optional HTTP transport, SHA-256 checksums for release artifacts, and Sigstore keyless signing for installer artifacts.
crypto_call_justification: The project does not reimplement cryptographic algorithms. It calls platform/tooling implementations such as Node.js crypto, sha256sum, and Sigstore cosign where cryptographic functions are needed.
crypto_floss_justification: Cryptographic tooling used by the project is FLOSS, including Node.js/OpenSSL, sha256sum, and Sigstore cosign.
crypto_keylength_justification: The gateway does not manage long-term cryptographic keys or define configurable key lengths. Sigstore keyless signing is delegated to Sigstore/Fulcio/Rekor and GitHub Actions OIDC.
crypto_working_justification: The project does not depend on broken algorithms such as MD4, MD5, single DES, RC4, or Dual_EC_DRBG for security. Release verification uses SHA-256 and Sigstore.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: The project does not depend on SHA-1, CBC-mode SSH, or similarly weak cryptographic modes for its own security mechanisms.
crypto_pfs_justification: The project does not implement its own key-agreement protocol. Network transport security for hosted project sites and downloads is provided by HTTPS endpoints.
crypto_password_storage_justification: The gateway does not store user passwords. Provider credentials stay in the user's CLI/provider environment and are not persisted by the gateway.
crypto_random_justification: Where randomness is needed in code paths, the project uses platform cryptographic APIs such as Node.js crypto randomUUID/randomBytes rather than insecure pseudo-random generators.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source code, npm package, and GitHub release artifacts are delivered over HTTPS. Installer artifacts include SHA256SUMS and Sigstore bundles.
delivery_unsigned_justification: The release process publishes SHA256SUMS and Sigstore bundles for installer artifacts. README.md and SECURITY.md document verifying SHA256SUMS.sigstore.json with cosign before trusting artifact checksums.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: The release commit had green security and dependency checks. The repository uses Dependabot, OSV Scanner, npm audit via scripts/release-security-audit.sh, gosec, govulncheck, opengrep, and gitleaks. No public unpatched medium-or-higher project vulnerability older than 60 days is currently recorded.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No open critical project vulnerability is currently recorded. SECURITY.md defines a coordinated disclosure process and the security workflow runs on push and pull_request.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: gitleaks runs in .github/workflows/security.yml and .gitleaks.toml is checked in. The security workflow also includes an attribution guard to prevent future Claude/Anthropic co-author trailers.
static_analysis_justification: OSS SAST runs in .github/workflows/sast.yml: opengrep against a vendored ruleset for JavaScript/TypeScript, plus gosec and govulncheck for Go. The security workflow also runs actionlint, zizmor, shellcheck, OSV Scanner, gitleaks, typos, and lychee; eslint-plugin-security runs in CI.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: opengrep, gosec, govulncheck, eslint-plugin-security, OSV Scanner, gitleaks, actionlint, and zizmor cover common vulnerability classes in TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, shell, dependencies, secrets, and GitHub Actions workflows.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: The security and sast workflows fail on findings when they run on push and pull_request (opengrep, gosec, and govulncheck are all blocking gates; the installer's initial gosec findings were triaged, with two fixed and the remainder justified in-line via #nosec). Recent release commits have successful security, SAST, Code Quality, OpenSSF Scorecard, CI, Dependency Graph, and Dependabot Updates runs.
static_analysis_often_justification: Static analysis runs on push and pull_request in the sast and security workflows. The sast workflow and Scorecard also run on schedules.
dynamic_analysis_justification: The project runs dynamic execution tests with Vitest, integration tests that exercise MCP flows, CLI handler tests, HTTP transport tests, async-job tests, pack smoke tests, and server-start smoke tests in CI.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: The project does not produce C or C++ software. The Go installer is built with Go's memory-safe runtime, and the primary gateway is TypeScript/JavaScript.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Vitest assertions are enabled throughout the test suite, and CI runs the assertion-based test suite on every push and pull request.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: CI fails if tests, build, lint, format, or security audit fail. Confirmed dynamic test failures are fixed before release or merge.
general_comments: Initial OpenSSF Best Practices evidence for llm-cli-gateway. This JSON is intended to prefill the BadgeApp project entry; an authenticated maintainer must still review and save the passing-badge form at bestpractices.dev.
